[2020-12-01 14:02] terrareveene (Terra Reveene): Just throwing the idea out there because I haven't seen anybody else mention it: Add exhaust to cryo ray's click ability, such that you can't use the same ray to freeze multiple turns in a row.
Say you have and use 3 cryo rays to freeze wall to kill engis. Once you do this, the cryo rays become exhausted and can't be used the following turn. 
Your opponent will then have a turn where they can prepare for their next turn where the cryo rays will become active again.
[2020-12-01 14:04] megasupp (mega-supp): I think this use of cryo rays is core to its design and produces interesting decisions. I really like Masn's idea of making it so cryo ray loses HP every turn but also when you click it
[2020-12-01 14:06] velizar_ (Name): Exhaust of 1 instead of a limit to 3 uses sounds very curious
[2020-12-01 14:06] megasupp (mega-supp): that could potentially lead to some silly situations where you are cluster BP and buy cryo ray to prevent opponent from sniping gauss charges under construction
[2020-12-01 14:06] megasupp (mega-supp): because you would have to make cryo ray quite beefy
[2020-12-01 14:09] silentslayers: I dont think the cryo one really adds much gameplay compared to lifespan (just a tiny bit), but the gauss fab example he gave is actually meaningful to gameplay. Some BP builds really can get f'ed just because when gauss fab is at 8 LS and it gets killed immediately it's painful.
[2020-12-01 14:13] masn6811 (Masne98888): it changes the decision between "do you kill the fab" and "when do you kill the fab"
[2020-12-01 14:18] megasupp (mega-supp): I would really like to avoid  bloating the numbers too much. numbers >20 just aren't aesthetically pleasing
[2020-12-01 14:18] megasupp (mega-supp): but I guess 24 fab is fine if it makes the unit more interesting
[2020-12-01 14:19] p0lari (Polari): counterpoint: big numbers are awesome and it's not like we're currently in danger of having too many of them
[2020-12-01 14:19] p0lari (Polari): what's even double digits, tant, fab, zemora and that one new unit?
[2020-12-01 14:19] megasupp (mega-supp): I'm just afraid that it will make some games feel like a very elaborate exercise in arithmetic

Cryo Ray 1G - 3:Fragile:
Click: pay 1 :Fragile: to chill 1, exhaust 2
Adding exhaust to cryo ray might be interesting, as an alternative way of nerfing it slightly. 
In situations where you could chill with them to deny absorb, you'll now decrease the pressure for your opponent on their following turn. It could also open up situations where chilling to deny absorb might be the incorrect move (or where you could sort of 'gambit granularity' or otherwise just gambit against it, since it's less likely that a follow-up breach will happen while the cryos are exhausted).

--- Thread (50 messages, 2020-12-03) ---
[2020-12-03 12:31] silentslayers: __**Unit Change Propositions for Friday (Community Collaborated):**__

**Fission Turret:** Reduce 1 lifespan, click now gives GGGG instead of GGG.

Notes: Fission turret shouldn't be such an efficient attacker when it's a small green unit. Especially considering it's efficient even when it's not clicked. The extra green from the click should help make this nerf feel a bit less dramatic. 

**Lancetooth:** Cost is now 8BX instead of 6BXX.

Notes: Lancetooth is too strong of a blue attacker when denying 2 absorb. Having efficient attack ramping and being in the color of defense simultaneously felt too easy of a tech choice. Lancetooth is still a good purchase when denying 1 absorb, but should be less desirable to go for and spam than currently, and should punish high economy lines less often.

**Scorchilla:** Scorchilla's HP is now 2.

Notes: Scorch is a pretty set dominating unit, because of its efficiency as an initial attacker, and its ability to deny absorb. The fact that it can also absorb 2 felt like icing on the cake it didn't need, so this should make it a bit less powerful. No strong opinion on whether fragile or not.

**The Wincer:** Costs 1 extra red.

Notes: Chrono filter wincer is a 2 unit combo which leads to many autowins and brainless openers. This will immediately remedy that. It also reduces the strength of mobile animus + wincer, which is also very strong. Specific combos aside, it also slightly nerfs Wincer, although not really by that much since the red is usually not spent on great things. You now wouldn't be able to fit in a rhino alongside your Wincer purchase, which might give some rushes a better chance. Not fitting in the tarsier doesn't feel too disastrous since Wincer players really like drones to be able to continue to spend their large amount of tech pre and post firing Wincer.
[2020-12-03 12:31] velizar_ (Name): also you should clarify that you want the barrier change to affect all barriers generated by units - lifespan and build time of units generated by other units aren't the same as lifespan and build time of units which are bought directly - for example front brooder builds frostbites with buildtime 1 and lifespan 3
[2020-12-03 12:34] velizar_ (Name): everything listed above sounds worth trying out IMO
[2020-12-03 12:34] velizar_ (Name): Fission Turret - does this change make you click turrets immediately instead of waiting their lifespan, if the set has cluster bolt or something else?
[2020-12-03 12:36] silentslayers: I think only in some circumstances, usually not.
[2020-12-03 12:37] velizar_ (Name): cluster bolt is basically 3 attack; since fission won't attack next turn, that makes it 2 attack instead; worth attacking for +2 next turn at the cost of losing 1 attack/turn one turn earlier? my intuition says no
[2020-12-03 12:37] silentslayers: It's already correct to do that now too situationally
[2020-12-03 12:37] silentslayers: I mentioned this also but no one cared
[2020-12-03 12:37] velizar_ (Name): it's not necessarily a bad thing
[2020-12-03 12:37] silentslayers: the energy is still annoying to gather
[2020-12-03 12:37] silentslayers: **Cryo ray:** HP changed to 2. Now has lifespan 5. Clicking no longer removes health.

Notes: Cryo ray was too strong for a green trinket. You were never in a hurry to get value from it in the past, since it already forced defense, and usually you could manage to deny some absorb without any huge downside. With this change, you're now putting more pressure on your opponent to stop you from exploiting your defenses since it doesn't cost you HP to use the cryos, and you no longer will end up just leaving cryos on 2 or 1 HP forever to maintain its threat.
[2020-12-03 12:37] silentslayers: Can someone check that over?
[2020-12-03 12:39] megasupp (mega-supp): I'm not a huge fan of cryo change. I think clicking cryos should still cost you something to provide interesting decisions

messages.author: silentslayers
messages.content:
__**Unit Change Propositions for Friday (Community Collaborated):**__

**Fission Turret:** Reduce 1 lifespan, click now gives GGGG instead of GGG.

Notes: Fission turret shouldn't be such an efficient attacker when it's a small green unit. Especially considering it's efficient even when it's not clicked. The extra green from the click should help make this nerf feel a bit less dramatic. 

**Lancetooth:** Cost is now 8BX instead of 6BXX.

Notes: Lancetooth is too strong of a blue attacker when denying 2 absorb. Having efficient attack ramping and being in the color of defense simultaneously felt too easy of a tech choice. Lancetooth is still a good purchase when denying 1 absorb, but should be less desirable to go for and spam than currently, and should punish high economy lines less often.

**Scorchilla:** Scorchilla's HP is now 2.

Notes: Scorch is a pretty set dominating unit, because of its efficiency as an initial attacker, and its ability to deny absorb. The fact that it can also absorb 2 felt like icing on the cake it didn't need, so this should make it a bit less powerful. No strong opinion on whether fragile or not.

**The Wincer:** Costs 1 extra red.

Notes: Chrono filter wincer is a 2 unit combo which leads to many autowins and brainless openers. This will immediately remedy that. It also reduces the strength of mobile animus + wincer, which is also very strong. Specific combos aside, it also slightly nerfs Wincer, although not really by that much since the red is usually not spent on great things. You now wouldn't be able to fit in a rhino alongside your Wincer purchase, which might give some rushes a better chance. Not fitting in the tarsier doesn't feel too disastrous since Wincer players really like drones to be able to continue to spend their large amount of tech pre and post firing Wincer.

messages.author: silentslayers
messages.content:
**Cryo ray:** HP changed to 2. Now has lifespan 5. Clicking no longer removes health.

Notes: Cryo ray was too strong for a green trinket. You were never in a hurry to get value from it in the past, since it already forced defense, and usually you could manage to deny some absorb without any huge downside. With this change, you're now putting more pressure on your opponent to stop you from exploiting your defenses since it doesn't cost you HP to use the cryos, and you no longer will end up just leaving cryos on 2 or 1 HP forever to maintain its threat.
